item.title: [Infraestrutura] CVE-2026-4342 - Nginx
item.description:
🚨 CVE-2026-4342 - Nginx
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: Nginx
Fonte: NVD
CVSS: 8.8
Resumo:
A security issue was discovered in ingress-nginx where a combination of Ingress annotations can be used to inject configuration into nginx. This can lead to arbitrary code execution in the context of the ingress-nginx controller, and disclosure of Secrets accessible to the controller. (Note that in the default installation, the controller can access all Secrets cluster-wide.)
Link: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-4342

item.title: [Segurança] CVE-2026-23250 - Check Point
item.description:
🚨 CVE-2026-23250 - Check Point
 
Criticidade: Média
Ferramenta: Check Point
Fonte: NVD
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: xfs: check return value of xchk_scrub_create_subord Fix this function to return NULL instead of a mangled ENOMEM, then fix the callers to actually check for a null pointer and return ENOMEM. Most of the corrections here are for code merged between 6.2 and 6.10.
Link: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-23250

item.title: [Infraestrutura] CVE-2026-32747 - Docker
item.description:
🚨 CVE-2026-32747 - Docker
 
Criticidade: Média
Ferramenta: Docker
Fonte: NVD
CVSS: 6.8
Resumo:
SiYuan is a personal knowledge management system. In versions 3.6.0 and below, the globalCopyFiles API eads source files using filepath.Abs() with no workspace boundary check, relying solely on util.IsSensitivePath() whose blocklist omits /proc/, /run/secrets/, and home directory dotfiles. An admin can copy /proc/1/environ or Docker secrets into the workspace and read them via the standard file API. An admin can exfi...
Link: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-32747

item.title: [Infraestrutura] CVE-2026-26939 - Elastic
item.description:
🚨 CVE-2026-26939 - Elastic
 
Criticidade: Média
Ferramenta: Elastic
Fonte: NVD
CVSS: 6.5
Resumo:
Missing Authorization (CWE-862) in Kibana’s server-side Detection Rule Management can lead to Unauthorized Endpoint Response Action Configuration (host isolation, process termination, and process suspension) via CAPEC-1 (Accessing Functionality Not Properly Constrained by ACLs). This requires an authenticated attacker with rule management privileges.
Link: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-26939

item.title: [Sistemas / Cloud] CVE-2026-2512 - WordPress
item.description:
🚨 CVE-2026-2512 - WordPress
 
Criticidade: Média
Ferramenta: WordPress
Fonte: NVD
CVSS: 6.4
Resumo:
The Code Embed plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via custom field meta values in all versions up to, and including, 2.5.1. This is due to the plugin's sanitization function `sec_check_post_fields()` only running on the `save_post` hook, while WordPress allows custom fields to be added via the `wp_ajax_add_meta` AJAX endpoint without triggering `save_post`. The `ce_filter()` function th...
Link: https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-2512

item.title: [Sistemas / Cloud] USN-8018-3: Python 2.7 vulnerabilities
item.description:
🚨 USN-8018-3: Python 2.7 vulnerabilities
 
Criticidade: Baixa
Ferramenta: Ubuntu
Fonte: https://ubuntu.com/security/notices/rss.xml
CVSS: 2.7
Resumo:
USN-8018-1 fixed CVE-2025-12084, CVE-2025-15282, CVE-2026-0672, CVE-2026-0865 for python3. This update provides the corresponding updates for python2.7. Original advisory details: Denis Ledoux discovered that Python incorrectly parsed email message headers. An attacker could possibly use this issue to inject arbitrary headers into email messages. This issue only affected python3.6, python3.7, python3.8, python3.9, py...
Link: https://ubuntu.com/security/notices/USN-8018-3

item.title: [Sistemas / Cloud] USN-8103-2: Exiv2 regression
item.description:
🚨 USN-8103-2: Exiv2 regression
 
Criticidade: Baixa
Ferramenta: Ubuntu
Fonte: https://ubuntu.com/security/notices/rss.xml
CVSS: 0.0
Resumo:
The update caused a regression for Ubuntu 20.04 LTS, Ubuntu 22.04 LTS, Ubuntu 24.04 LTS and Ubuntu 25.10. Original advisory details: It was discovered that Exiv2 did not correctly handle reading certain buffers. An attacker could possibly use this issue to leak sensitive information. This issue only affected Ubuntu 16.04 LTS and Ubuntu 18.04 LTS. (CVE-2020-18771) Wen Cheng discovered that Exiv2 did not correctly hand...
Link: https://ubuntu.com/security/notices/USN-8103-2

item.title: [Segurança] Magento PolyShell Flaw Enables Unauthenticated Uploads, RCE and Account Takeover
item.description:
💣 Magento PolyShell Flaw Enables Unauthenticated Uploads, RCE and Account Takeover
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: Qualys
Fonte: The Hacker News
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
Sansec is warning of a critical security flaw in Magento's REST API that could allow unauthenticated attackers to upload arbitrary executables and achieve code execution and account takeover. The vulnerability has been codenamed PolyShell by Sansec owing to the fact that the attack hinges on disguising malicious code as an image. There is no evidence that the shortcoming has been exploited in
Link: https://thehackernews.com/2026/03/magento-polyshell-flaw-enables.html

item.title: [Segurança] Russian hackers exploit Zimbra flaw in Ukrainian govt attacks
item.description:
💣 Russian hackers exploit Zimbra flaw in Ukrainian govt attacks
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: SentinelOne
Fonte: BleepingComputer
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
Hackers part of APT28, a state-backed threat group linked to Russia's military intelligence service (GRU), are exploiting a Zimbra Collaboration Suite (ZCS) vulnerability in attacks targeting Ukrainian government entities.
Link: https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/russian-apt28-military-hackers-exploit-zimbra-flaw-in-ukrainian-govt-attacks/

item.title: [Segurança] 54 EDR Killers Use BYOVD to Exploit 35 Signed Vulnerable Drivers and Disable Security
item.description:
💣 54 EDR Killers Use BYOVD to Exploit 35 Signed Vulnerable Drivers and Disable Security
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: Qualys
Fonte: The Hacker News
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
A new analysis of endpoint detection and response (EDR) killers has revealed that 54 of them leverage a technique known as bring your own vulnerable driver (BYOVD) by abusing a total of 35 vulnerable drivers. EDR killer programs have been a common presence in ransomware intrusions as they offer a way for affiliates to neutralize security software before deploying file-encrypting malware.
Link: https://thehackernews.com/2026/03/54-edr-killers-use-byovd-to-exploit-34.html

item.title: [Sistemas / Cloud] Unknown attackers exploit yet another critical SharePoint bug - theregister.com
item.description:
💣 Unknown attackers exploit yet another critical SharePoint bug - theregister.com
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: Office
Fonte: Google News RSS
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
A publicação menciona exploração ativa, técnica ofensiva ou abuso envolvendo Office. Consulte o link para entender melhor o impacto e as recomendações.

item.title: [Segurança] Apple Warns Older iPhones Vulnerable to Coruna, DarkSword Exploit Kit Attacks
item.description:
💣 Apple Warns Older iPhones Vulnerable to Coruna, DarkSword Exploit Kit Attacks
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: Qualys
Fonte: The Hacker News
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
Apple is urging users who are still running an outdated version of iOS to update their iPhones to secure against web-based attacks carried out via powerful exploit kits like Coruna and DarkSword. These attacks employ malicious web content to target out-of-date versions of iOS, triggering an infection chain that leads to the theft of sensitive data.
Link: https://thehackernews.com/2026/03/apple-warns-older-iphones-vulnerable-to.html

item.title: [Infraestrutura] From Disruption to Modernization: Streamlining Ingress NGINX to Avi Transition with Avi Conversion Tool (ACT)
item.description:
📰 From Disruption to Modernization: Streamlining Ingress NGINX to Avi Transition with Avi Conversion Tool (ACT)
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: VMware
Fonte: https://blogs.vmware.com/feed
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
The Kubernetes Project announced in November 2025 that the open-source Ingress NGINX controller is being retired, an announcement that immediately impacted platform engineering teams globally. As the deprecation deadline is this month (March 2026), crucial support, security patches, and critical bug fixes will cease suddenly. The deadline is rapidly creating understandable anxiety among platform teams …
Link: https://blogs.vmware.com/load-balancing/2026/03/19/ingress-nginix-to-avi-transition-with-act/

item.title: [Segurança] How CISOs Can Survive the Era of Geopolitical Cyberattacks
item.description:
📰 How CISOs Can Survive the Era of Geopolitical Cyberattacks
 
Criticidade: Alta
Ferramenta: SentinelOne
Fonte: BleepingComputer
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
Geopolitical tensions are driving destructive cyberattacks designed to disrupt operations, not demand ransom. CISOs must limit lateral movement and contain breaches to reduce the impact of wiper campaigns.
Link: https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/how-cisos-can-survive-the-era-of-geopolitical-cyberattacks/

item.title: [Infraestrutura] Deploy Elastic in air-gapped and disconnected environments
item.description:
📰 Deploy Elastic in air-gapped and disconnected environments
 
Criticidade: Baixa
Ferramenta: Elastic
Fonte: https://www.elastic.co/blog/feed
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
Elastic is a unified data analytics platform designed to support search, security, and observability solutions in completely air-gapped and disconnected environments.
Link: https://www.elastic.co/blog/deploy-elastic-air-gapped-disconnected-environments

item.title: [Infraestrutura] Why your DIY Kubernetes stack won’t survive the era of agentic AI
item.description:
📰 Why your DIY Kubernetes stack won’t survive the era of agentic AI
 
Criticidade: Baixa
Ferramenta: ESX
Fonte: https://blogs.vmware.com/feed
CVSS: N/D
Resumo:
This blog was originally published on TheNewStack. We are at an inflection point in enterprise IT. For the last decade, “modernization” has been synonymous with containerization — essentially packaging applications so they can run anywhere. The challenge is no longer just about running …
Link: https://blogs.vmware.com/tanzu/why-your-diy-kubernetes-stack-wont-survive-the-era-of-agentic-ai/
